Real-time communication has become an essential part of modern applications. To strengthen my backend development skills, I built NexTalk, a real-time chat platform that combines instant messaging with AI-powered features using PHP, MySQL, and the Google Gemini API.
📌 Project Overview
NexTalk is a responsive communication platform where users can register, authenticate securely, create conversations, exchange messages instantly, participate in polls, and receive AI-powered assistance.
Unlike a simple chat application, NexTalk was designed with scalability, security, and clean architecture in mind.
✨ Features

• 🔐 Secure User Authentication

• 💬 Real-Time Messaging

• 🤖 AI Chat Assistant (Google Gemini)

• 👥 Conversation Management

• 📊 Poll Creation & Voting

• 👤 Role-Based Access Control

• ⚡ Server-Sent Events (SSE)

• 📱 Responsive User Interface

• 🗄️ MySQL Database Integration
🛠️ Technologies Used

• PHP

• MySQL

• HTML5

• CSS3

• JavaScript

• Google Gemini API

• Git & GitHub

• XAMPP

• phpMyAdmin
🚧 Challenges
The biggest challenge was implementing reliable real-time communication while integrating AI-powered responses. I faced synchronization issues where new messages weren’t appearing immediately for users.
After debugging the API flow and improving the Server-Sent Events implementation, the messaging became much smoother and more responsive.
This project taught me a lot about backend architecture, debugging, API integration, authentication, and building scalable web applications.
📚 What I Learned

• Building REST-style APIs in PHP

• Database design using MySQL

• Authentication and Authorization

• AI API Integration

• Real-time communication with Server-Sent Events

• Clean project structure

• Debugging large web applications

• Git and collaborative development
🔗 Project Repository
GitHub:
https://github.com/tahaahmad-max/NexTalk-Real-Time-Chat-Platform
Thank you for reading! I’m always excited to build projects that combine modern web technologies with AI to solve real-world problems.
